instructions: (Claude Code's built-in system prompt; see --bare mode docs)

queries.user_text:
You're debugging a real-codebase bug in `libucl-6` (project `libucl`, an open-source C/C++ project from the BugsC++ corpus).

The buggy source tree is on the host at `/Users/ibraheemamin/projects/COS484/chatdbgpro/data/workspaces/libucl-6/libucl/buggy-6` and is also mounted at `/work` inside a Linux/amd64 container named `bench-t4-c5e3e350dd4747fcaac9` (gdb, the buggy binary, and all build deps live in that container; the binary is NOT runnable on this host directly).

Buggy binary inside the container: `/work/tests/.libs/test_basic`
Failing test invocation:           `/work/tests/.libs/test_basic ../tests/basic/11.in ../tests/basic.out`
Observed behavior:                  `exit_code:2`
Bug type:                          `other`
Language:                          `c`

How to investigate
------------------
* To READ source code, use Read / Grep / your normal tools — the source tree is at `/Users/ibraheemamin/projects/COS484/chatdbgpro/data/workspaces/libucl-6/libucl/buggy-6` (it's been added via `--add-dir`).
* To RUN the binary, run gdb, or execute anything else inside the build environment, use Bash with this template:

      docker exec -i -w /work bench-t4-c5e3e350dd4747fcaac9 bash -c '<cmd>'

  e.g.
      docker exec -w /work bench-t4-c5e3e350dd4747fcaac9 bash -c 'ls -la'
      docker exec -w /work bench-t4-c5e3e350dd4747fcaac9 bash -c 'gdb -batch -ex run -ex bt --args /work/tests/.libs/test_basic ../tests/basic/11.in ../tests/basic.out'

  The container is dedicated to this case; it'll be torn down when this session ends, so don't worry about cleanup.

Final answer
------------
Identify the root cause in the source, propose both a local fix and a structural global fix.

Your final response MUST include three labelled paragraphs:

  ROOT CAUSE: <file:line and what is wrong>
  LOCAL FIX:  <minimal code change>
  GLOBAL FIX: <structural change preventing this CLASS of bug>

Do NOT modify the source files on disk. Just investigate and produce the diagnosis as your final assistant message.

tool_calls.call: docker exec -w /work bench-t4-c5e3e350dd4747fcaac9 bash -c '/work/tests/.libs/test_basic ../tests/basic/11.in /tmp/out.txt; echo "Exit code: $?"'
docker exec -w /work bench-t4-c5e3e350dd4747fcaac9 bash -c 'LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/work/src/.libs /work/tests/.libs/test_basic ../tests/basic/11.in /tmp/out.txt; echo "Exit code: $?"'

docker exec -w /work bench-t4-c5e3e350dd4747fcaac9 bash -c 'LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/work/src/.libs /work/tests/.libs/test_basic ../tests/basic/11.in /tmp/out.txt 2>&1; echo "Exit code: $?"; echo "---Output file---"; cat /tmp/out.txt 2>/dev/null || echo "(no output file)"'

docker exec -w /work bench-t4-c5e3e350dd4747fcaac9 bash -c 'LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/work/src/.libs strace -e trace=open,openat,read,write,exit_group /work/tests/.libs/test_basic ../tests/basic/11.in /tmp/out.txt 2>&1 | tail -50'
docker exec -w /work bench-t4-c5e3e350dd4747fcaac9 bash -c 'LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/work/src/.libs /work/tests/.libs/test_basic ../tests/basic/11.in /tmp/out.txt 2>&1 | xxd | head -20'

docker exec -w /work bench-t4-c5e3e350dd4747fcaac9 bash -c 'rm -f /tmp/out.txt; LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/work/src/.libs /work/tests/.libs/test_basic ../tests/basic/11.in /tmp/out.txt; echo "Exit code: $?"; ls -la /tmp/out.txt; file /tmp/out.txt'
